PRESENTER deck (live-demo backdrop, ~7 slides). Sparse and bold: at most one headline claim and ONE number per slide, minimal body text, big takeaways the speaker narrates over. NOT a dense handout.

Pitch the 'metal-kernel-opt' (P7) project for the Applied track. The strategy to win is framing this as the truest extension of the original SIA paper: bringing their flagship GPU-kernel optimization to Apple Silicon and Metal. Focus on the hardcore engineering loop where the agent rewrites actual Metal Shading Language source code (a fused RMSNorm kernel), JIT-compiles it via FastMetalKernel, and is scored purely on verifiably faster microseconds-per-op execution. Reinforce the trust model by highlighting the external, frozen golden oracle used for correctness (the candidate never sees it). Money-shot slide: the speed delta climbing across SIA generations.
